bull.t: Sustained Revenue Scaling & High Gross Margin
bull.b: Multi-year top-line expansion and consistently healthy gross margins indicate strong unit economics and scalable demand for prostate cancer tests. This scale supports fixed-cost absorption and creates a realistic path to operating leverage and eventual profitability if SG&A growth is managed.

Successful integration of acquired sales teams and customer migration materially increases sales productivity and enables cross-selling of a broader prostate cancer menu. Durable commercial efficiencies can raise lifetime customer value and lower incremental customer acquisition costs over time.

Building an AI data platform and embedding AI endpoints into a major clinical trial strengthens clinical differentiation and long-term defensibility. AI-driven prognostic improvements can increase guideline adoption, payer acceptance, and price/margin resilience over multiple years.

bear.b: Sharp debt increases and negative equity undermine financial flexibility and raise refinancing and covenant risk. Structural leverage reduces ability to invest in commercial expansion or R&D without external funding and increases dilution or financing costs over the medium term.

Persistent negative operating and free cash flow means the business remains reliant on external capital to fund operations and integration. Continued cash burn elevates dilution risk and can constrain strategic investments until consistent positive FCF is achieved.

Exiting Resolve reduces revenue diversification and triggers restructuring costs, while a contested Medicare recoupment and a $15M earn‑out materially cut the cash buffer. This combination heightens execution and liquidity risk during integration and commercialization of core products.
